text:
If you like it sunny and comfortable, today's the day to be outside, because the humidity climbs back in fast starting tomorrow. Expect a bright, easy sky with a high near 82 and barely a breath of wind, the kind of July day that doesn't ask anything of you. The lake's in beautiful shape too, water's a swimmable 73, waves around a foot, wind light and variable, basically flat all day and into tonight.

Monday turns the corner: sunny start, high near 88, and stickier air moving in. The models mostly agree there, though the Euro is running a touch warmer, so don't be shocked if it feels closer to 90 by afternoon. That warm, humid trend keeps building into Tuesday, when a sharp cold front arrives with a real chance of strong to severe thunderstorms, possibly overnight, so if you're camping or traveling Tuesday night, keep half an eye on the sky.

Today, though, just enjoy it: good beach and boating window right through sunset near 8:30, and I'd have zero hesitation planning an evening on the water.
packet:
OBSERVED (2026-07-12T10:40:00+00:00): Clear, 59F feels 59F, humidity 82%, wind ENE 3 mph
NWS FORECAST:
  Today: Sunny, with a high near 82. North wind 1 to 5 mph.
  Tonight: Mostly clear, with a low around 64. Southeast wind 2 to 7 mph.
  Monday: Mostly sunny, with a high near 88. Southwest wind 5 to 8 mph.
  Monday Night: Partly cloudy, with a low around 70. Southwest wind 3 to 8 mph.
AFD KEY MESSAGES (forecaster's reasoning, issued 2026-07-12T08:20:00+00:00):
  1. Seasonable conditions today will trend more uncomfortable to start next week with increasing temperatures and humidity.
  2. Precipitation chances increase early week. A sharp cold front will pass through the region late Tuesday into Wednesday bringing a chance for thunderstorms, some of which could be strong to severe.
  3. Forecast trends suggest seasonable conditions, but the pattern predictability appears relatively low.
AFD WHAT CHANGED: As of 411 AM EDT Sunday... The Storm Prediction Center has placed areas along the International Border in a Day 3 Slight Risk for the potential for severe damaging wind gusts late Tuesday into Wednesday.
LAKE (broad waters):
  Today: Light and variable winds. Sunny. Waves around 1 foot.
  Tonight: Light and variable winds, becoming south 5 to 10 knots. Mostly clear. Waves 1 foot or less.
  Monday: South winds 5 to 15 knots, becoming west. Partly sunny. Waves 1 foot or less.
  Monday Night: West winds 10 knots or less, becoming south. Partly cloudy. Waves 1 foot or less.
LAKE GAGE: water 73F, level 96.57 ft (normal)
MODEL SPREAD (where forecasts diverge):
  2026-07-12: highs [GFS 82, Euro 82, ICON 82] spread 0F; precip chance [GFS 0%, Euro 0%, ICON 0%]
  2026-07-13: highs [GFS 84, Euro 88, ICON 84] spread 4F; precip chance [GFS 6%, Euro 4%, ICON 0%]
  2026-07-14: highs [GFS 91, Euro 98, ICON 99] spread 8F; precip chance [GFS 45%, Euro 12%, ICON 15%]
AIR: AQI 46 Good (PM2.5) — On Sunday, July 12th, expect generally good air quality in Vermont.
SUN: rise 2026-07-12T05:20-04:00, set 2026-07-12T20:36-04:00, UV max 7.45
WEATHER UNDERGROUND / WEATHER.COM:
  high 85, low 63: Plenty of sun. Highs in the mid 80s and lows in the low 60s.
  high 89, low 70: Mix of sun and clouds. Highs in the upper 80s and lows in the low 70s.
WCAX METEOROLOGIST: A First Alert Weather Day continues for Tuesday. Hazy, hot and humid conditions are expected with highs ranging from the upper 80s to mid-90s. Those areas that reach the mid-90s could have a heat index around 100 degrees. Also, we’re watching a cold front that could bring a few severe thunderstorms during the evening or overnight. Though the main threat will be in northern parts of the region, nocturnal thunderstorms are more likely to catch people off guard, especially if you’re camping or traveling. Stay tuned for updates. Any thunderstorms will exit the region by Wednesday morning.
NBC5 FIRST WARNING (2026-07-11T22:38:00Z): Stupendous Sunday as low humidity and sunshine continue — Highs soar into the 90s early this week Stupendous Sunday as low humidity and sunshine continue Highs soar into the 90s early this week
